text:
Nikoraurusu (ニコラウルス, Nikoraurusu) is the Captain of the Guards for 9 th Prince Halkenburg. Tweet by Togashi, retrieved 17 Dec 2024 https://x.com/un4v5s8bgsvk9xp/status/1868697918624219482 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Appearance

Nikoraurusu is a man of average height and build, with very short, dark hair and an oval face shape. He has large eyes, thick eyebrows, and a sharp, angular nose, and is generally expresses his emotions clearly on his face. He also has sideburns.

Like the rest of Halkenburg's personal guards, Nikoraurusu wears a suit while acting in his capacity as a guard.

Personality

Like most of Halkenburg's men, Nikoraurusu happily serves him and wants the best for him. When Halkenburg rushes out of his room after his unnatural collapse on the first day of the voyage, Nikoraurusu smiles at him and asks him how he feels. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 After Room 1009's moment of silence on the third day, when Halkenburg begins to move in great sadness, Nikoraurusu is the one to proactively ask Halkenburg where he is going, and also the one to remind him that what he is doing is against the established rules, which could get him arrested. Still, when Nikoraurusu sees Halkenburg's face, full of sadness, he falls silent in appreciation of the depth of Halkenburg's motivations, and does not further try to stop him, only following him in silence. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 On the tenth day of the voyage, Nikoraurusu appears indignant at Halkenburg's arrest by the Justice Bureau, and seemingly tries to protest it or get the Justice Bureau to compromise somehow. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

On the first day of the voyage, 9 th Prince Halkenburg retires from the departure ceremony back to his room, Room 1009, and is horrified to discover that at least eleven of his men, including Nikoraurusu, have collapsed in his room and are seemingly unconscious. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 Halkenburg awakes in his bed several hours later and rushes out to check on his men. He sees his men busy unpacking and organizing his possessions. Fellow guard Furichosefu comments that Halkenburg is awake, and Nikoraurusu asks Halkenburg how he feels. Halkenburg wonders if what he saw was a dream, but by chance, looks at the back of his left hand and sees a pinion imprinted there. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

On the third day of the voyage, Halkenburg leads his men in a moment of silence for 12 th Prince Momoze. His men stand in a semicircle surrounding Halkenburg; Nikoraurusu stands on Anesuto's right. The unison of Halkenburg's men in action generates a massive amount of aura, strong enough for 1 st Prince Benjamin to feel from four rooms away in Room 1001 and contact a terrified Shikaku, a personal elite soldier of his who had been sent to guard and spy on Halkenburg on the first day, for an immediate report of the situation. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

After the moment of silence ends, Halkenburg begins walking toward Room 1009's entrance. Nikoraurusu asks Halkenburg where he is going, and when he hears that Halkenburg plans to make another appeal to his father, King Nasubi, reminds him that seeing the King outside the banquets is forbidden. Halkenburg continues anyway, wanting to appeal even if it disqualified him, and Nikoraurusu tries to appeal to Halkenburg again, reminding him that Nasubi had declared that the battle for succession cannot be canceled, and highlighting that further resistance could get him arrested. Halkenburg turns and looks Nikoraurusu in the face, and with tears in his eyes, says that another Prince becoming a victim with him having done nothing means that he might as well have killed them himself. Nikoraurusu is unable to respond, and slowly follows behind Halkenburg as he walks to Room 1009's door. Halkenburg leaves with five guards, and instructs the rest of them, including Nikoraurusu, to continue carrying out their duties. Shikaku follows along. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

Halkenburg makes his way to Nasubi's residence but is not allowed to enter, and settles for leaving the guards at the VVIP gate a letter with instructions to pass it to him. He continues trying this for the next few days, and on the sixth day, forces the guards at the VVIP gate to let him in at gunpoint. Halkenburg speaks to Nasubi, and after failing to appeal to Nasubi, to assassinate Nasubi, and to commit suicide, returns to his room, awakened and resolved to win, and equipped with a new ability. Halkenburg returns to Room 1009 and uses his Grimmal: The Dissonance to force the soul of his guard Sumidori into Shikaku's body. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

On the seventh day of the voyage, The exact time this occurred is ambiguous, and is most likely on Day 6 or 7, but the possibility remains that it occurs on Day 8. While originally surrounded by completely unrelated events confirmed to take place on Day 9, information released in Chapter 404 about the workings of consciousness of those affected by Halkenburg's ability makes it illogical for the complete interaction to happen on Day 9. A wide-angle panel of the Black Whale is also noted in the middle of Halkenburg's scene in Chapter 386, which has been very commonly used throughout the arc to denote that an unspecified amount of time has passed. Halkenburg and his men stand around a deeply-sleeping Sumidori, with a machine hooked up to him to monitor his brainwaves. The men wonder what happened to Shikaku's soul, and Halkenburg gives them four possibilities. After some discussion about the difficulties in proving or disproving any of the theories, Halkenburg calls for his men's attention, and explains his understanding of why he was given the ability, and the implications that the reason he was given the ability has on what the ability itself can do. Nikoraurusu stands on the right side of the group, between Anesuto and Kontasutin, and the men listen closely to Halkenburg. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the ninth day, Halkenburg's men gather again, to collectively review their investigation and to be briefed on the next steps to take. "Shikaku" walks over to Room 1007, and presses its doorbell. When one of 7 th Prince Luzurus' guards speaks to him over the intercom, "Shikaku" proclaims Benjamin, and promptly shoots himself in the head. While Benjamin and Luzurus' teams scramble to make sense of the situation, Halkenburg has his men surround "Sumidori", prepared to identify who is inside Sumidori's body and progress their investigation. "Sumidori" wakes up, and identifies Halkenburg in front of him. 

On the tenth day of the voyage, Benjamin and Balsamilco Might, his Captain of the Guards, hear Vict, another of their men, struggle in battle again Halkenburg. Following this, Balsamilco has Halkenburg arrested under suspicion of premediated murder, evidenced by Benjamin's soldiers continually experiencing anomalies. IPA members Steiner and Peuckert, temporarily on loan to Kakin's Justice Bureau Investigation Department, turn up in a team of five at Room 1009 to effect the arrest. A member of their team speaks with Nikoraurusu and Anesuto and inform them what is going on. Nikoraurusu and Anesuto are naturally unhappy at the situation, but Halkenburg and his men are unable to do anything, and Halkenburg is led away by the Justice Bureau team. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

On the eleventh day of the voyage, Balsamilco turns up at the Justice Bureau in preparation for his trial against Halkenburg, not expecting any resistance since Halkenburg had been separated from his guards. However, after passing through security, Balsamilco notices Vict and is distracted by his presence, leading to him falling victim to Halkenburg's ability and swapping souls with Halkenburg himself. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 Halkenburg lets his body get infected with TSK-17 found on Balsamilco's body and, very early in the morning on the twelfth day, die. Through Balsamilco's body, Halkenburg conveys three final wishes to Benjamin: a ship-wide announcement, a send-off by his supporters, and his personal guards to transport his casket. Benjamin grants them, wanting to be able to show that he honored Halkenburg's final wishes. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

At noon on the twelfth day of the voyage, twelve of Halkenburg's men act as pallbearers for Halkenburg's casket; Nikoraurusu stands at the front of the right side of the casket. They begin the funeral procession from the Tier 3 General Hospital, where Halkenburg had been rushed to when he initially collapsed, Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 and are seen making their way up the central staircase connecting Tiers 2 and 3. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Given that Halkenburg sent his men Shedule and Yuhirai to Kurapika's Nen lessons on the second day with instructions to learn all they can about Nen beasts, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 it is likely that none of Halkenburg's men, including Nikoraurusu, know anything about Nen beyond what was mentioned by Kurapika over the emergency channel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

It is worth noting that Nikoraurusu is seen contributing to the massive aura when Halkenburg and his men unite in a moment of silence for Momoze. This is a sign that Nikoraurusu has been marked by Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast; Kurapika notes that those with the mark of Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast are half-awakened to Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Although Nikoraurusu has black hair, he is occasionally drawn without his hair shaded in. This happens in Chapter 362 and Chapter 366. He is also drawn without his sideburns in Chapter 386.
